label: 192_primaries_candidates_congressional primaries_primaries project
description: Brookings studied all candidates in congressional primaries from 2014 onwards, focusing on political polarization and the impact of factions like the Tea Party. The research highlighted how these factors influenced candidate selection and election outcomes.

earliest_articles.title: The Primaries Project: The Future of American Politics
earliest_articles.excerpt: Editor’s Note: This blog post is part of The Primaries Project series, where veteran political journalists Jill Lawrence and Walter Shapiro, along with scholars in Governance Studies, examine the congressional primaries and ask what they reveal about the future of each political party and the future of American…

newest_articles.title: How factional primaries could turn a Democratic wave into a trickle
newest_articles.excerpt: Over the next seven months, most states will hold primary elections to determine congressional candidates for their respective districts and states. There are two ways of analyzing midterm elections: the macro and the micro. The macro forces at work vary little from one midterm cycle to another. As my colleague…

newest_articles.title: Assessing the role of candidate quality in the 2026 midterms
newest_articles.excerpt: History suggests Democrats could benefit in the 2026 midterms. As William Galston explained in one of the first pieces in Brookings’ coverage, incumbent presidents almost always lose seats in midterm elections. Trump’s approval ratings are low, and Democrats have often performed better when he is not on the ballot.…

central_articles.title: What we know about the 2024 Democratic and Republican parties: An analysis of congressional candidates
central_articles.excerpt: In 2014, Brookings began studying all the candidates running for Congress through The Primaries Project. Unlike many studies of congressional elections in political science and unlike most journalists’ coverage of congressional elections, the Brookings studies focused on all candidates who got themselves on the…

central_articles.title: Lessons from the 2022 Primaries – what do they tell us about America’s political parties and the midterm elections?
central_articles.excerpt: The author would like to thank research analyst Andy Cerda, for his contributions to the Primaries Project. The importance of factions.
